questions.question: 1. Given T = 300 K, m<sub>n</sub> = 1.08 × 10<sup>-31</sup> kg, m<sub>p</sub> = 0.56 × 10<sup>-31</sup> kg, h = 6.626 × 10<sup>-34</sup> J·s, E<sub>F</sub> = 0.4 eV, E<sub>C</sub> = 0.2 eV, and E<sub>V</sub> = 0.1 eV, what are the electron concentration n0 and hole concentration p0?

answers.a: n0 = 7.3 x 10<sup>26</sup> m, p0 = 1.1<sup>-3</sup> x 10<sup>18</sup> m<sup>-3</sup>
answers.b: n0 = 2.5 x 10<sup>26</sup> m, p0 = 3.0<sup>-3</sup> x 10<sup>18</sup> m<sup>-3</sup>
answers.c: n0 = 4.1 x 10<sup>26</sup> m, p0 = 1.8<sup>-3</sup> x 10<sup>18</sup> m<sup>-3</sup>
answers.d: n0 = 5.2 x 10<sup>26</sup> m, p0 = 2.7<sup>-3</sup> x 10<sup>18</sup> m<sup>-3</sup>

explanations.a:
Given the formulas for the electron and hole concentrations in a semiconductor:

n0 = NC e^F, NC = 14 (2m_n k_B T / h^2)^(3/2), F = (E_F - E_c) / (k_B T)
p0 = NV e^F, NV = 14 (2m_p k_B T / h^2)^(3/2), F = (E_V - E) / (k_B T)
explanations.b:
Given the formulas for the electron and hole concentrations in a semiconductor:

n0 = NC e^F, NC = 14 (2m_n k_B T / h^2)^(3/2), F = (E_F - E_c) / (k_B T)
p0 = NV e^F, NV = 14 (2m_p k_B T / h^2)^(3/2), F = (E_V - E) / (k_B T)
explanations.c:
Given the formulas for the electron and hole concentrations in a semiconductor:

n0 = NC e^F, NC = 14 (2m_n k_B T / h^2)^(3/2), F = (E_F - E_c) / (k_B T)
p0 = NV e^F, NV = 14 (2m_p k_B T / h^2)^(3/2), F = (E_V - E) / (k_B T)
explanations.d:
Given the formulas for the electron and hole concentrations in a semiconductor:

n0 = NC e^F, NC = 14 (2m_n k_B T / h^2)^(3/2), F = (E_F - E_c) / (k_B T)
p0 = NV e^F, NV = 14 (2m_p k_B T / h^2)^(3/2), F = (E_V - E) / (k_B T)

questions.question: 2. From the above values find intrinsic carrier concentration

explanations.a: intrinsic carrier concentration is the square root of n0*p0
explanations.b: intrinsic carrier concentration is the square root of n0*p0
explanations.c: intrinsic carrier concentration is the square root of n0*p0
explanations.d: intrinsic carrier concentration is the square root of n0*p0

questions.question: 3. At higher temperatures T>400K, in a doped n-type semiconductor, where would the Fermi level be?

answers.d: In the middle of conduction and valance band

explanations.a: Recall what happend to carrier concentration at high temp.
explanations.b: Fermi level is closer to conduction band for ptype semiconductor.
explanations.c: No. That implies Ev is negative!!
